# Quality Gate Requirements Checklist: System Testing Quality Gate

- **Purpose**: Validate that the quality-gate requirements are complete, clear, measurable, and ready for implementation planning.
- **Created**: 2026-06-12
- **Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `$speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the required quality gate result states fully defined, including pass, fail, not applicable, accepted risk, environment failure, missing data, and unstable check? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, FR-007, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all v1 buyer critical journeys listed with enough detail to determine required coverage boundaries? [Completeness, Spec FR-002]
- [ ] CHK003 Are all v1 seller critical journeys listed with enough detail to determine required coverage boundaries? [Completeness, Spec FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 Are all v1 admin critical journeys listed with enough detail to determine required coverage boundaries? [Completeness, Spec FR-004]
- [ ] CHK005 Are service-level capabilities scoped clearly enough to separate route ownership, authorization, messaging, workflow, payment, media, and notification coverage? [Completeness, Spec FR-005]
- [ ] CHK006 Are documentation-only and planning-only change requirements sufficiently defined for a narrower quality decision? [Completeness, Spec FR-010]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "shared baseline checks" defined with criteria that distinguish baseline checks from journey-specific checks? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-012, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "release readiness" defined clearly enough to decide when full critical-path coverage is required? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "affected journeys" defined clearly enough to support impact-based gating without subjective interpretation? [Clarity, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "blocking failure" defined clearly enough to distinguish merge blocking from release blocking? [Clarity, Spec User Story 1, FR-011]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "non-production data and controlled external-provider behavior" defined clearly enough to prevent unsafe interpretation? [Clarity, Spec FR-008, Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are accepted-risk requirements consistent between the clarification log, functional requirements, and key entity definition? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications, FR-011, Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK013 Are impact-based gating requirements consistent between the contributor scenario, functional requirements, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec User Story 1, FR-012, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK014 Are unstable-check requirements consistent between acceptance scenarios, edge cases, and functional requirements? [Consistency, Spec User Story 3, Edge Cases, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK015 Are the success criteria consistent with the first-version scope of critical paths rather than exhaustive system coverage? [Consistency, Spec SC-001, Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can every success criterion be objectively evaluated without choosing implementation tools or commands? [Measurability, Spec SC-001 to SC-006]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the 30-minute merge-readiness target scoped to the same impact-based gate described in requirements? [Measurability, Spec SC-002, FR-012]
- [ ] CHK018 Is the 95% unambiguous-result target defined against clear result states and not dependent on manual interpretation? [Measurability, Spec SC-003, FR-001]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the 50% post-merge regression reduction criterion tied to an observable baseline period or release comparison? [Gap, Spec SC-005]
- [ ] CHK020 Is the 10-minute stakeholder review target tied to clearly defined coverage and gap information? [Measurability, Spec SC-006, FR-009]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are primary contributor, stakeholder, and maintainer scenarios each traceable to functional requirements? [Traceability, Spec User Stories, FR-001 to FR-013]
- [ ] CHK022 Are alternate scenarios defined for documentation-only, planning-only, and narrowly impacted runtime changes? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1, FR-010, FR-012]
- [ ] CHK023 Are exception scenarios defined for product behavior failure, environment failure, missing test data, and unstable checks? [Coverage, Spec User Story 3, FR-007, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK024 Are release-specific scenarios defined for full critical-path coverage and release-owner accepted risk? [Coverage, Spec FR-011, FR-012]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK025 Are unavailable dependency, incomplete seed data, external provider, shared behavior, documentation-only, and deferred coverage edge cases all reflected in requirements? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are recovery expectations defined for inconsistent check results after the single allowed rerun? [Edge Case, Spec FR-013]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements defined for coverage gaps discovered during stakeholder review before release? [Edge Case, Spec User Story 2, FR-009]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements defined for critical-path checks that are intentionally deferred for a release? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, FR-009, FR-011]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK029 Are reliability requirements for unstable checks measurable and distinct from ordinary product behavior failures? [Reliability, Spec FR-007, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK030 Are privacy and safety requirements clear for customer data, payment credentials, and customer-impacting notifications? [Security/Privacy, Spec FR-008, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK031 Are observability requirements defined for reporting affected journey, expected outcome, failure category, and blocking decision? [Observability, Spec FR-006, User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK032 Are performance expectations for contributor and stakeholder decision time measurable and aligned with gate scope? [Performance, Spec SC-002, SC-006]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK033 Are assumptions about unchanged APIs, events, and service responsibilities sufficient to prevent accidental catalog or boundary changes? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK034 Are dependencies on the API catalog, event catalog, service documentation, and architecture overview traceable to coverage expectations? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK035 Are external-provider limitations documented clearly enough for later planning to choose safe substitutes or controlled behavior? [Dependency, Spec Edge Cases, FR-008]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK036 Are terms such as "critical-path area", "shared baseline checks", "release readiness", and "accepted coverage gap" defined consistently enough for planning? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-006, FR-009, FR-012]
- [ ] CHK037 Are there any conflicts between impact-based gating and the requirement for 100% v1 critical journey coverage? [Conflict, Spec FR-012, SC-001]
- [ ] CHK038 Are approving roles for accepted risk distinct enough from implementation team roles that planning can assign ownership without redefining the spec? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-011]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`.
- Add comments or findings inline when a requirement needs correction.
- Items evaluate the quality of written requirements, not implementation behavior.
